intro.uz: AJ Hogeʼning kuchli eʼtiqodi: muvaffaqiyatsizlik degan narsa yoʻq — faqat natija (feedback) va oʻrganish tajribasi bor. Watt haqidagi POV hikoyada mototsikl 110 marta ishlamaydi, ammo har safar u biror narsa oʻrganadi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi shu gʻoyani tilga aylantiradi: aralash shart gap (oʻtmish sharti → hozirgi natija: If I hadn't failed, I would be…) va sabab-natija-maqsad bogʻlovchilari (because / so / therefore / in order to).
intro.en: AJ Hoge's core belief: there is no such thing as failure — only feedback and a learning experience. In the POV story about Watt, the motorcycle fails 110 times, yet each time he learns something. This week's two grammar topics turn that idea into language: mixed conditionals (past condition → present result: If I hadn't failed, I would be…) and cause–result–purpose connectors (because / so / therefore / in order to).

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻtmishdagi bir voqea bugungi holatingizga qanday taʼsir qilganini aralash shart gap bilan ayta olasiz (If I hadn't …, I wouldn't be …) va fikringizni because / so / therefore / in order to bilan mantiqan bogʻlab, ravon tushuntira olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can say how a past event shaped who you are today using a mixed conditional (If I hadn't …, I wouldn't be …), and connect your ideas logically with because / so / therefore / in order to.

grammar.titleUz: Aralash shart gap: oʻtmish sharti → hozirgi natija
grammar.titleEn: Mixed conditionals — past condition, present result
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — discuss how past events shape the present
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can link a past condition to a present result (had + V3 → would + verb)
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tili ikkala qismni ham bir xil “-ganimda … boʻlardim” qolipi bilan beradi va zamonlarni ajratmaydi. Ingliz tili esa shartni oʻtmishga (had + V3), natijani hozirga (would + sof feʼl) qatʼiy ajratadi: “Agar xato qilmaganimda, hozir bilmasdim” = “If I hadn't made mistakes, I wouldn't know this now”.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ If I didn't fail, I am strong now. ❌ If I hadn't failed, I wouldn't have been strong now. ✅ If I hadn't failed, I wouldn't be strong now. Oʻtmish sharti — had + V3; hozirgi natija — would + sof feʼl (would have + V3 emas, chunki natija hozirda).

examples.en: If I hadn't lost my job, I wouldn't be a teacher now.
If Watt hadn't tried again, he wouldn't have his motorcycle today.
examples.uz: Watt yana urinmaganda, bugun uning mototsikli boʻlmasdi.

examples.en: If I hadn't started English, I wouldn't be here right now.
examples.uz: Ingliz tilini boshlamaganimda, hozir bu yerda boʻlmasdim.

exercises.prompt: If I ___ failed so many times, I wouldn't be this strong today.
If she had learned from her mistakes, she ___ more confident now.
exercises.hintUz: hozirgi natija → would + sof feʼl (would be), oʻtmish emas

exercises.prompt: If Watt ___ given up, he wouldn't be riding his Harley now.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda rost gap ayting (ovoz chiqarib): oʻtmishdagi bir voqeani bugungi holatingizga bogʻlang — “If I hadn't …, I wouldn't … now.”
exercises.hintUz: if + had + V3 → would + sof feʼl (natija hozirda)

grammar.titleUz: Sabab, natija, maqsad bogʻlovchilari: so / therefore / in order to
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 3 — build a coherent argument (cause → effect)
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can link cause, result and purpose with because / so / therefore / in order to
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida sabab ham, natija ham koʻpincha bitta “-gani uchun / shuning uchun” bilan beriladi va bogʻlovchi jumla oxirida turadi. Ingliz tilida esa: because sababni ergashtiradi (because it exploded), so natijani ikki jumla oʻrtasida bogʻlaydi (it exploded, so…), in order to esa maqsadni beradi (+ sof feʼl).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ I practise every day for improve my English. ❌ He tried again for learn. ✅ I practise every day in order to improve my English. ✅ He tried again in order to learn. Maqsad uchun for + feʼl EMAS, balki in order to + sof feʼl (yoki so that + gap).

examples.en: The bike exploded, so he beat himself up.
examples.uz: Mototsikl portladi, shuning uchun u oʻzini koyidi.

examples.en: He learned from every mistake; therefore, there was no failure.
examples.uz: U har bir xatodan oʻrgandi; shuning uchun muvaffaqiyatsizlik boʻlmadi.

examples.en: He kept trying in order to build a better bike.
examples.uz: Yaxshiroq mototsikl yasash uchun urinishda davom etdi.

exercises.prompt: The first bike wouldn't start, ___ he tried again.
exercises.hintUz: natija (shuning uchun) → so, ikki jumla oʻrtasida

exercises.prompt: He studied the feedback ___ improve his design.
exercises.hintUz: maqsad → in order to + sof feʼl (improve)

exercises.prompt: He didn't beat himself up ___ he saw it as a learning experience.

exercises.promptUz: Oʻzingiz haqingizda bitta voqeani uch xil bogʻlang (ovoz chiqarib): “I … , so … . I … because … . I did it in order to … .”
exercises.hintUz: so = natija, because = sabab, in order to = maqsad

vocab.uz: qaytar aloqa; ishdan keyin keladigan natija/maʼlumot
vocab.defEn: information or a result that comes back to you after an action
vocab.example: There is no failure, only feedback.

vocab.defEn: to add or stick something on, e.g. a name onto an experience
vocab.example: We tack on the label 'failure' afterwards.

vocab.defEn: a series of actions that continues over time
vocab.example: Happiness is a process, not a thing.

vocab.defEn: to compare two things to see how they differ
vocab.example: Contrast two boys learning to ride a bicycle.

vocab.defEn: to drop something or be clumsy
vocab.example: He fumbled around and dropped his tools.
Finally he said, 'I get it! I know how to do it!'

vocab.en: to get something out of it
vocab.defEn: to gain a benefit or learn something from an experience
vocab.example: The second time, he got something out of it.
Don't beat yourself up over a mistake.

vocab.defEn: to be going smoothly and steadily with no problems
vocab.example: My business is cruising along nicely.

vocab.defEn: an event you learn something from, rather than a failure
vocab.example: Being fired was a great learning experience for me.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda Charli ishini yoʻqotib, qarzga botgan va Korleonedan yordam soʻraydi — ammo rad javob oladi. “No Failure” darsiga mos: AJ ham bir vaqtlar ishdan boʻshatilgan, lekin buni muvaffaqiyatsizlik emas, saboq deb qabul qilgan. Sizningcha, Charli oʻz ahvolini “muvaffaqiyatsizlik” deb qabul qiladimi yoki undan biror saboq ola oladimi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue Charlie has lost his job and fallen into debt, so he asks Corleone for help — but he is turned down. It fits the 'No Failure' theme: AJ was once fired too, yet he treated it as learning, not failure. Do you think Charlie will see his situation as failure, or get a learning experience out of it?

keyVocab.defEn: a period of time when the economy of a country is bad

keyVocab.uz: qattiq zarba bermoq, ogʻir taʼsir qilmoq
keyVocab.defEn: to have a strong, negative effect on someone
the amount of money you owe to a person, bank or company
besides that; in addition to everything else
to request money that you will pay back later

quiz.q: How much electronic waste is thrown away every year globally?
quiz.qUz: Har yili dunyo boʻyicha qancha elektron chiqindi tashlanadi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: c) 42 million tonna — 2015-yilgi BMT hisobotiga koʻra. Catherine javobni oldindan bilardi. Bu raqam yildan-yilga oshib bormoqda, chunki biz “throwaway culture” (bir marta ishlatib tashlab yuborish madaniyati)da yashaymiz.

vocab.defEn: a small piece of technology; a gadget

vocab.uz: barqaror, uzoq davom eta oladigan (zarar yetkazmaydigan)
vocab.defEn: able to continue over a long period without causing harm
to join two things with a screw / to undo a screw
to reduce what you believe or hope will happen
no longer valid, useful or up to date
a culture in which we throw things out very easily instead of repairing them

funEnglish.title: There Is No Failure, Only Feedback — a mindset that changes everything

speakingPrompt.en: Think of a time something went wrong for you. What did you learn from it — and how would your life be different now if it hadn't happened? Explain the cause, the result, and what you did on purpose to move forward.
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: aralash shart gap (If I hadn't …, I wouldn't be … now) va sabab-natija-maqsad bogʻlovchilari (because / so / therefore / in order to).
